attacking_style: Pure sprinter who times final acceleration for 100-250m to go, preferring to follow wheels rather than lead out. Vulnerable when forced to open sprint early (multiple premature launches at 250-750m documented).
team_tactics: Relies heavily on organized leadout trains (BORA-Hansgrohe historically, UAE later). Tactical execution deteriorates without full team support — multiple wins came with minimal support but consistent failures when team infrastructure present but poorly coordinated.
terrain_preference: Flat technical finishes with barriers to exploit late gaps. Exposed on climbing finishes (premature accelerations on gradients), dropped on rolling stages with penultimate climbs, and vulnerable in crosswind echelon racing.
vulnerability: Headwinds force him to lead out early and fade badly. Crosswinds catch him behind requiring energy-sapping chases. Positioning errors in technical circuits when lacking organized leadout. Pure speed insufficient without tactical positioning.
key_patterns: Goes too early repeatedly: documented premature sprints at 250-750m in 2022, 2019, 2018 (6+ occasions)
Won from compromised positions by threading barrier-side gaps, exceptional bike handling in technical/wet conditions (4 instances)
Team dependency paradox: won solo after 10km self-leadout and with minimal support, but faded when full trains present
Wheelsucker vulnerability: beaten by followers when forced to wear points jersey and lead (Tour de Suisse, Guangxi)
Form resurgence 2023 Giro: first stage win since 2019, revealed previous underperformance was team resource issue
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Giro win with virtually no team support suggests career revival after 2020-2022 lean years, though early 2023 Classics form still questionable
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 13
